items.caption: A slide from a presentation, focusing on the topic of "notary stamp or seal." it contains text and an illustration. the text explains that a notary seal is an embossed seal or ink stamp affixed to a legal document by a notary public. it also mentions that state notaries must have their own notary seal or stamp with their name, and that the notary is responsible for safeguarding and destroying the seal/stamp once he/she ceases to be a notary. the slide also notes that 10 u.s.c. §1044a specifically exempts federal military notaries from the requirement to place an official seal or stamp on documents they notarize. it further states that as a matter of good practice, 10 u.s.c. §1044a notaries should use a department of the navy/marine corps embossed seal or stamp, in addition to the notary authorization language. the slide also includes a note that 1044a notary's legal office, with oic approval, may purchase the embosser/stamp as an office asset (fiscal justification) needed to further the legal assistance mission.
